core_mechanism: supervised estimation of per-element weights in an additive linear scoring function over bag-of-token counts, with the weights fit by regressing feature frequencies onto outcome labels — and the resulting accuracy governed by a signal-to-input-volume tradeoff that shifts the optimal input granularity as the number of independent training targets changes with aggregation level
brief_reason: The abstract's method is a 'supervised lexicon-learning approach' that trains scores against return and volatility labels (learning per-word weights of an additive score from labeled outcomes), and its central result attributes the reversal of which input performs best to 'the interaction between document volume and the amount of independent training signal available at each level of aggregation.'
